mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a declining federal funds rate amplifies consumer spending power, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS rises but FEDFUNDS also rises, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term equity momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases correlate with negative SPX 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 320, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 320, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for real yields.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts alongside low unemployment signal synchronized strength in consumer confidence and labor markets, boosting real estate and financial sector earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when HOUST rises but UNRATE simultaneously increases, or the interaction effect yields a negative out-of-sample Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth confirms broad-based economic expansion, improving corporate profit margins and equity risk premiums, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant when adjusted for lagged market momentum.
